evidence: wrapHandler in WordSketchApiServer establishes a clear contract: IAE→400, IOException→500, Exception→500 — this is the intended baseline for all handlers

VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial wraps its entire body in catch(Exception)→500, preventing wrapHandler's IAE→400 path from ever firing for this endpoint; a malformed JSON body returns 500 instead of 400, unlike all sibling POST handlers

ExplorationHandlers has its own inner try-catch blocks for IOException and IAE inside each handler method (e.g. handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti) — these produce correct 400/500 responses but create a double-handling layer since wrapHandler already provides the same mapping

BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations wraps IllegalArgumentException as IOException: `throw new IOException("Invalid CQL pattern...", e)` — this reclassifies a client-side error (bad pattern) as an I/O error; wrapHandler will then return 500 instead of 400 for this class of input problem

CollocateQueryHelper.executeCollocateSearch catches InvalidQuery (a library parse exception) and rethrows as IOException — this is consistent with the IOException-based I/O boundary contract of the QueryExecutor interface
ConlluConverter.convertConlluToWplChunks lets IOExceptions propagate naturally; Main.handleBlackLabIndexCommand catches Exception at the top level — consistent for a CLI boundary
GrammarConfigLoader.load throws IOException for config parse errors — consistent with file-I/O framing

strengths: wrapHandler provides a single documented error-classification contract for all registered HTTP endpoints — IAE as 400, IOException as 500, any other Exception as 500
QueryExecutor interface uniformly declares `throws IOException` so all callers have a single exception type to handle from the query layer
BlackLabSnippetParser methods that fail to parse return null or empty string rather than throwing, keeping parse errors local and non-fatal
error_consistency.issue_character: The errors are concentrated at two boundaries: a visualization handler that opts out of the wrapHandler classification contract, and a query-layer wrapping of IllegalArgumentException as IOException that promotes a client error to a server error.
error_consistency.score_rationale: The codebase has a clearly designed error strategy (wrapHandler contract, IOException boundary in QueryExecutor) but it is unevenly applied. The VisualizationHandlers outlier breaks the 400/500 classification the project clearly intends, and the IAE-wrapped-as-IOException in findCollocations silently misclassifies a correctible client error. These are not catastrophic but they are real inconsistencies that would frustrate a developer debugging HTTP error codes. The score sits below 70 because the defects are in the API boundary — the highest-visibility layer — even though the core query layer is internally consistent.

issues.summary: VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial catches all exceptions as 500, bypassing wrapHandler's IAE→400 contract
evidence: `catch (Exception e) { sendError(exchange, 500, ...)` wraps the entire handler body
JSON parsing of malformed `width`/`height` fields would throw NumberFormatException (subclass of IllegalArgumentException via fastjson) and be reported as 500 rather than 400
SketchHandlers.handleBcqlQueryPost and all ExplorationHandlers handlers correctly propagate IAE or return explicit 400 responses for client errors
issues.suggestion: Remove the broad inner try-catch from handleVisualRadial; add specific validation for required fields (items array null check, invalid width/height) that throws IllegalArgumentException to be caught by wrapHandler as 400

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations wraps IllegalArgumentException (bad CQL pattern) as IOException, causing wrapHandler to return 500 for a client-side input error
evidence: `catch (IllegalArgumentException e) { throw new IOException("Invalid CQL pattern...", e); }` in findCollocations
The wrapping IOException reaches wrapHandler which maps IOException→500, losing the 400 classification that the original IAE would have triggered
The original IAE comes from buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution when a CQL pattern does not start with '[' — this is detectable and correctable by the API caller
issues.suggestion: Let the IllegalArgumentException propagate unchanged from findCollocations so wrapHandler classifies it as 400; or rethrow as a domain-specific checked exception that the handler layer can distinguish; alternatively add explicit pattern validation in the API handler before calling findCollocations

issues.summary: ExplorationHandlers inner try-catch blocks duplicate the error classification already provided by wrapHandler, creating redundant error-handling layers
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore has `catch (IOException e) { sendError(500, ...) } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) { sendError(400, ...) }` inside the handler
wrapHandler already provides the same IOException→500 and IAE→400 mapping for every registered endpoint
The inner catches produce the same HTTP codes as wrapHandler but with slightly different error message formatting — a future change to the error message format would need to be made in two places
issues.suggestion: Remove the inner try-catch blocks from handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti; let IOException and IAE propagate to wrapHandler which provides the same classification uniformly; keep explicit sendError only for cases that need a specific 400 message before the exception phase (e.g. missing-seeds check)

root_causes: VisualizationHandlers was written before the wrapHandler pattern was standardised, and was never updated to remove its own catch-all
likely_symptoms: double-handled-exploration-errors is partly a symptom of the wrapHandler contract being introduced incrementally rather than designed upfront
